params.name: Otis Steam Excavator
params.description: The Otis Steam Excavator, designed and manufactured by American inventor Otis, is the ancestor of modern excavators. This machine was steam-powered and mounted on a railway flatcar, a semi-rotary, rail-mounted single-bucket excavator primarily used for railway construction. Although Otis died young, his steam excavator was widely used, becoming an important tool for railway construction, canal excavation, and open-pit mining in the United States. Its appearance marked the first time humans used  …
